description_long: This object is a vintage-style microphone with a cylindrical, ribbed metal grille housing the pickup element, reminiscent of classic broadcast microphones from the mid-20th century. The grille is vertically oriented and features horizontal slats, providing both protection and an iconic aesthetic. The microphone is mounted on a sturdy, round metal base via a straight cylindrical stem, ensuring stability during use. The finish is a dark, slightly weathered metallic gray, suggesting durability and a utilitarian design. There are no visible buttons, switches, or branding, emphasizing its streamlined, functional appearance. This type of microphone would typically be found in urban environments such as radio stations, music venues, or public speaking areas.
description: A vintage-style, ribbed metal microphone on a round, sturdy base with a dark metallic finish and no visible controls or branding.
description_view_0: The microphone is seen from the side, showing the ribbed grille and the supporting stem attached to the round base.
description_view_1: The front view highlights the horizontal slats of the grille and the symmetrical, upright design of the microphone.
description_view_2: Another side view, nearly identical to view 0, showing the ribbed grille and the supporting stem from the opposite side.
description_view_3: The rear view displays the back of the grille and the supporting structure, with the base and stem clearly visible.
